title: Lessingianthus psilophyllus
intro: Lessingianthus psilophyllus, commonly known as Piquiá-de-terra or Piquiá-branca, is a small, shrubby plant native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lessingianthus psilophyllus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, coughs, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Lessingianthus psilophyllus is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the tribe Vernonieae. Lessingianthus psilophyllus is further classified into the genus Lessingianthus. The species is native to Brazil, and its classification is part of the broader understanding of the plant's evolutionary history. The classification of Lessingianthus psilophyllus reflects its relationships with other plants within the Asteraceae family.
